emblems.emblem_desc: Based on meta trends in early 2026, Thamuz is played as an aggressive EXP lane fighter focusing on attack speed, sustain, and true damage. The 2026 emblem setups prioritize high sustain and damage output to leverage his Grand Lord Lava passive.

hero.hero_overview: Thamuz is a high-sustain, aggressive EXP Lane fighter in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang known for dealing massive True Damage, high attack speed, and exceptional 1v1 capability. He relies on his scythes to deal continuous damage and his ultimate for HP regeneration. He is best played in the EXP lane for early game domination.
hero.hero_strength: Thamuz is a formidable Fighter in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, primarily played in the EXP Lane or as a Jungler. He is renowned for his oppressive early-game presence, high sustained damage, and immense lifesteal, often considered one of the best 1v1 heroes in the game.
hero.hero_weakness: Thamuz is a formidable Fighter in Mobile Legends: Bang Bang, primarily played in the EXP Lane or as a Jungler. He is renowned for his oppressive early-game presence, high sustained damage, and immense lifesteal, often considered one of the best 1v1 heroes in the game.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Angela is the best partner for Tamuz because her ultimate provides a massive shield and movement speed. She allows you to dive into the enemy team and survive their burst damage. Her slow skills help you to catch mobile targets and trigger your passive more often. With Angela inside you Tamuz becomes a truly unstoppable monster in every teamfight.

Tigreal is a perfect match because he can group up multiple enemies with his ultimate skill. This allows Tamuz to deal massive AOE true damage to the entire enemy team at once. His crowd control gives you enough time to position yourself and activate your own ultimate safely. Working together with a good tank makes your job in the front line much easier.

Estes provides incredible healing that keeps Tamuz at full health during long battles. Since Tamuz needs to stay in the middle of the fight his high HP pool works perfectly with constant regeneration. This combination allows you to ignore enemy tower damage and dive deep for kills. You can win almost any 2v5 situation if Estes is supporting you from behind.

counters.counter_desc: Valir is your worst nightmare because he can push you away constantly with his skills. His slow and stun effects make it impossible for Tamuz to reach the enemy backline. Even with your ultimate active you will struggle to deal any damage if he is nearby. Always try to wait for him to use his push before you jump into the fight.

Baxia has a built in anti heal effect that significantly reduces your life steal during the ultimate. He is very tanky and can soak up your true damage while slowing you down with his shield. This makes him a very dangerous opponent for Tamuz in any teamfight situation. You should avoid 1v1 duels with him unless you have a significant gold lead.

Karrie deals percentage based true damage that ignores your high HP and physical defense. She can melt down Tamuz in a few seconds especially in the late game. Her high mobility with the dash skill makes it very hard for you to catch her. Focus on killing her first or wait for your teammates to provide crowd control.

dominants.dominant_desc: Always remember to pick up your scythes after throwing them to reduce the cooldown of your first skill. This mechanic is essential for maintaining constant true damage and fast jungle clearing. Use your second skill to jump over walls and surprise enemies from the bushes. When your Ultimate is active stay in the middle of the fight to maximize your HP regeneration. Do not waste your skills on minions if an enemy hero is nearby as you need them for defense.

Focus on fast jungle clearing to reach level four as soon as possible. Use your first skill to harrass the enemy jungler and contest the Turtle. Tamuz is very strong early so do not be afraid of 1v1 fights. Always keep your scythes moving to maintain your passive burn damage.

This is your power spike after buying Corrosion Scythe. Start ganking side lanes and focus on taking down turrets. Use your Ultimate during teamfights to soak up damage and heal back instantly. Stay aggressive and control the enemy forest to deny their farm.

In the late game you should act as a frontliner for your team. Target the enemy marksman or mage and dive deep using your second skill. Be careful of heavy crowd control and wait for the right moment to engage. Your main job is to create space for your team and take the Lord.

emblems.emblem_desc: Fighter Emblem provides the necessary HP and physical defense for a jungler. Swift talent increases your attack speed to trigger the passive more often. Festival of Blood is the core of this build for extra spell vamp. Every kill increases your healing from skills throughout the game. Brave Smite restores four percent of your max HP when dealing skill damage. This combination makes Tamuz incredibly hard to kill in teamfights.

equips.equip_desc: Start with basic boots and then rush Corrosion Scythe for immediate damage. This is the core item to trigger your passive and slow down enemies. Balanced build provides a great mix of attack speed and survivability. Tough Boots and the last two defensive items are always situational. This setup is the most reliable choice for most matches.

hero.hero_overview: Tamuz is an aggressive fighter with massive true damage and outstanding lifesteal. In the jungle role, he can clear camps incredibly fast and dominate the early game. His goal is to dive into the heat of battle, soak up damage, and burn down squishy targets, preventing them from escaping.
hero.hero_strength: Early Game Dominance. His passive and low skills cooldowns allow him to out farm most junglers. True Damage. His passive ignores enemy armor making him a threat even to tanks. Insane Regeneration. His Ultimate restores HP with every attack. Objective Control. He can solo Turtle and Lord very quickly.
hero.hero_weakness: Anti heal items. Items like Sea Halberd reduce his effectiveness. Kiting. It is difficult for Tamuz to reach enemies with high mobility. Ultimate Dependency. Without his Ultimate Tamuz is much less durable in teamfights. Falling off Late Game. Enemy marksmen can burst him down before he gets close.

data.recommend: This guide is perfect for players who want to dominate the jungle with Tamuz. It includes the most effective build for high survivability and true damage. Follow these tips to master early game aggres

spell.spell_desc: Flame Retribution is the best choice for maximum damage and winning duels. Use it to steal physical attack from the enemy and increase your own power. Ice Retribution is a great situational pick against mobile heroes with dash skills. It steals movement speed and helps Tamuz to stay close to targets. Tough Retribution is better against tanky drafts because it steals hybrid defense. Choose your retribution color based on the enemy team composition for the best results.

data.title: Tamuz Absolute Jungle King

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Angela gives Thamuz bonus health and movement speed, which makes him practically unkillable and faster. He becomes a nightmare that enemies can't escape. That said, Thamuz isn't a hero you desperately need to pair with Angela. He's a good option if the Angela ultimate is available and the jungler doesn't need this. But if your Roamer can pick a tank instead, they usually should pick it

counters.counter_desc:
Early game, before level 4, Cici absolutely destroys Thamuz. Focus on last-hitting minions to hit level 4 as fast as possible without taking too much poke or dying. Once you hit level 4, the matchup evens out a bit. After you get Corrosion Scythe, you can fight her comfortably
Strategy: Hit her with your first skill and engage. If you're out of range, you can use your dash, but it's better to save it if you don't need it. Pop your ultimate as soon as the fight starts and keep auto-attacking until she uses her dash to escape. Then, use your second skill to chase her down. As long as she's not massively ahead, Cici loses this matchup once you have your items

Before level 4, you can't do anything against Yu Zhong. After level 4, you can trade with him, but be careful if he already has War Axe. Once you get Corrosion Scythe, your auto-attacks give you a real chance. If Yu Zhong uses his ultimate, just disengage and don't fight him. Once you have War Axe and anti-heal (Dominance Ice), he's no longer a threat. After you have three core items, you will win the 1v1 against him if he won't use ultimate to escape

dominants.dominant_desc:
Use your first skill off cooldown. It pulls enemies and minions toward you, gives you a speed boost, and sets up your enhanced ranged basic attack for some safe poke or to close distance. Your second skill is your dash for mobility, chasing, or for resetting your first skill (remember the trick: use it before the scythe returns)
Before level 4, play safe against strong laners. Against weaker opponents like Argus, you can be more aggressive, zoning them and going for a kill if the opportunity arises
If the enemy turret has the shield and line is empty (you killed laner/laner on another line), always hit it to get the extra gold. You need to buy your Corrosion Scythe as soon as possible
Once you hit level 4, rotate to the Turtle if your jungler is there and help secure it. In lane, you should be able to hold your own against almost anyone as long as you're not behind
Once you have Corrosion Scythe, you're ready to fight and join team fights confidently. Remember: He's excellent against tanky lineups. In team fights, he thrives as a frontliner, using his ultimate to shred tanks and fighters while soaking up damage. The slow from the item combined with your auto-attacks makes you a kiting nightmare and a damage threat. If the enemy doesn't have a dash, they're probably dead. In the very late game, once you have your full build, you can sell your boots for Guardian Helmet

emblems.emblem_desc:
As a fighter, Thamuz works best with the Fighter emblem set

Swift: A good chunk of his damage comes from attack speed cause he autoattacker

Festival of Blood: Thamuz's whole playstyle revolves around sustain, so any extra regeneration is a huge help

Temporal Reign: The typical combo is to engage with your second skill, slow with your first, and then pop your ultimate. Temporal Reign helps get your first and second skills back up faster after ulting, which is perfect for him

equips.equip_desc:
Tough Boots: These help reduce the duration of crowd control effects, which is essential for staying in the fight

Corrosion Scythe: This item is a must-have. Since Thamuz is an auto-attacker, the slow from Corrosion Scythe makes him incredibly sticky, and enemies without a dash or Flicker simply can't get away

War Axe: This item amps up the healing he gets from dealing damage, which is especially powerful when his ultimate is active

Dominance Ice: Anti-heal is non-negotiable for any fighter. Dominance Ice makes him even more effective against tanks and fighters by reducing their sustain

Oracle: This amplifies his already strong regeneration, making him feel nearly unkillable while his ultimate is running

Queen's Wings: The passive on this item helps him survive longer in fights and also reduces the cooldown of his skills
Swapping boots for Guardian Helmet late game gives him a massive health pool, turning him into a true menace who can comfortably take on two or three enemies at once while his ultimate is up

hero.hero_overview: Thamuz is a decent B-tier fighter with strong regeneration and solid tank potential when built correctly. He's not a meta hero, and I wouldn't recommend maining him. There are simply stronger fighters out there like Yu Zhong, Lapu-Lapu, and others. That said, he's a great pick for beginners because his kit is straightforward. But even with his simplicity, there are some important nuances you need to understand to play him effectively
hero.hero_strength: He performs well at all stages of the game and has fantastic sustain and scaling health. With the right build and battle spell, he can deal significant damage while tanking and constantly healing himself. He's also great at slowing enemies, making it nearly impossible for them to escape if they've used their dash. In the exp lane, he holds his own in 1v1 matchups after level 4, only really struggling against Yu Zhong and Lapu-Lapu. His presence alone can zone out enemies. The enhanced basic attack after his first skill gives him a bit of extra range to close gaps. There's also a neat trick: if you use your second skill before the scythe from your first skill returns, your first skill instantly resets. His mobility is decent too, with a speed boost from his first skill and a dash on his second. He's excellent against tanky lineups. In team fights, he thrives as a frontliner, using his ultimate to shred tanks and fighters while soaking up damage, effectively acting as both a damage dealer and an off-tank
hero.hero_weakness: He's an auto-attack reliant hero. He's weak before level 4. He's completely dependent on his ultimate. If he falls behind in farm, he's easy to kill. Enemies can easily kite him on his ultimate. His biggest weaknesses in team fights are burst damage and crowd control. You need to have good timing with his ultimate, or you'll get blown up before you can even use it. At the Mythic Glory and Mythic Immortal levels, he struggles against experienced players who know exactly how to counter him

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game (Level 1-4): You are the strongest hero on the map. Don't be afraid to trade. Aggressively use Skill 1 to poke. You can often "cut the lane" (clear minions behind the enemy tower) to pressure the enemy jungler.
Mid Game: Focus on the backline. Your job is to dive the Mage or Marksman. Activate Vengeance + Ultimate and just stay on top of them.
Late Game: Thamuz falls off slightly in the very late game. Transition into a frontline "meat shield." Don't try to chase high-mobility heroes (like Joy or Nolan) alone; focus on zoning for your team.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Emblem: Fighter Emblem (Highest synergy due to Spell Vamp and Hybrid Defense).
Talent 1: Firmness / Agility (For extra defense or movement).
Talent 2: Festival of Blood (Crucial for scaling your sustain/Spell Vamp).
Talent 3: War Cry (Increases damage after 3 consecutive attacks—perfect for his high attack speed) or Brave Smite (for extra HP regen).

Mid Game: Focus on the backline. Your job is to dive the Mage or Marksman. Activate Vengeance + Ultimate and just stay on top of them.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: ✨ Angela's Ult gives Thamuz a huge shield, movement speed, and constant healing, making him truly unkillable while diving.
✨ Diggie's Ult grants CC immunity, removing Thamuz's biggest weakness and allowing him to dive fearlessly.
✨ Provide massive AOE CC to group enemies for Thamuz to dive into with his own Ult and AOE scythe damage.

counters.counter_desc: ✨ Karrie's True Damage passive shreds Thamuz's high HP build.
✨ Baxia's passive drastically reduces Thamuz's massive HP regen and lifesteal.
🔥 Mages with strong AOE or poke damage and knockback/disengage skills to keep Thamuz at an arm's length, preventing him from sticking and healing.

dominants.dominant_desc:
🔥 Combo Mastery: Use the sequence (S1) - (S2) - Enhanced Basic Attack - (S1) Reset - Enhanced Basic Attack to maximize damage and procs of your true damage passive.

🔥 Ultimate for Duel: Pop Cauterant Inferno (Ult) early in a duel to immediately gain HP, Attack Speed, and sustain. Don't save it.

🔥 Bully & Deny: Dominate the enemy laner. Clear the wave fast, then check the map to rotate to Turtle or invade the enemy jungle.

🔥 Scythe Zoning: Throw S1 to cut off the enemy's retreat path, forcing them to take damage/slow or change their movement.

🔥 Blink Safety: Save Chasm Trample (S2) to jump a wall and escape a gank. Only use it to initiate if you are confident you can secure the kill or survive the fight.

🔥 Dive the Backline: Your main target is the enemy Marksman or Mage. Use Flicker or S2 to bypass the frontline.

🔥 Instant Ult: Activate your Ultimate the moment you engage the backline. This ensures you survive the initial burst and start melting them with Attack Speed and HP regen.

🔥 Disrupt and Zone: Even if you can't get the kill, your relentless AOE damage and presence will force the enemy core to retreat, effectively zoning them out of the team fight.
dominants.dominant_title: 👑 TEAM FIGHT STRATEGIES (Mid to Late Game)

emblems.emblem_desc:
✨ This is the standard, high-sustain Thamuz build.

🔥 Festival of Blood: Provides crucial spell vamp, synergizing with his continuous Skill 1 damage and the area damage from his Ultimate.

🔥 Brave Smite: This is the most important talent. Whenever Thamuz damages an enemy hero with a skill (S1, S2, or Ultimate), he heals for a percentage of his Max HP. This perfectly complements his Ultimate's healing, making him incredibly hard to kill in sustained fights.

✨ Optimizes him for jungle clear speed and objective taking while retaining fight power

🔥 Seasoned Hunter: This talent is mandatory for any Jungler. It increases damage to Lord/Turtle and speeds up the entire farming process, which allows Thamuz to reach his item power spikes faster.

equips.equip_desc:
✨ Boots: Pick based on the enemy lineup (CC or Physical Damage).

✨ Corrosion Scythe: Rush this for lane dominance, Attack Speed, and the slow effect.

✨ War Axe: Complete this next to unlock high burst damage and Physical Pen for mid-game fights.

✨ Brute Force Breastplate: A great hybrid item that gives mobility, defense, and works perfectly with Thamuz's constant use of basic attacks and skills.

✨ Oracle: The cornerstone of your sustain. Buy this when the enemy's burst Magic Damage is becoming a threat and you need to amplify your HP regeneration.

✨Queen's Wings or Immortality: Finish with your preferred lategame insurance. Queen's Wings if you are dominating and need dueling power or Immortality if you need a reliable safety net.

hero.hero_overview:
👑 Background (Lore)

🔥 Known as the Lord of Lava, he is a powerful demon from the Abyss and one of its fiercest rulers, embodying pure destruction.

👑 Overview

🔥 Focus on Sustained Damage and Dueling: Excels in prolonged fights due to his high life steal and continuous damage.

🔥 Revamped Passive: Now features a Rage bar/mechanic that increases the chance to trigger his fiery explosion (True Damage) on basic attacks, making his damage more consistent.

🔥 Enhanced Survivability: His Ultimate, Cauterant Inferno, often grants an immediate burst of HP and enhances his attributes, making him a formidable tank-fighter in engagements.

🔥 Smoother Skill Usage: His first skill (Scythe Throw) and second skill (Lava Field) are often noted to feel faster or more impactful, improving his chase/escape potential and damage application.

👑 Role in the Game:

🔥 Primary Role: Fighter (High damage and durability).

🔥 Lane/Position: Primarily played in the EXP Lane (Sidelaner) due to his excellent dueling, wave clear, and sustain for split-pushing or holding the lane against most opponents.

🔥 Secondary Role: Can be played as a Jungler (using the Retribution battle spell) due to his fast clear speed and strong 1v1 potential for contesting objectives.

👑 Farming Routes:

🔥 EXP Lane (Primary):

✨ Focus on clearing the lane minion wave quickly to gain level and gold advantage (essential for his early power spike).

✨ Aggressively poke and zone the enemy laner.

✨ Rotate to secure the Gold Crab or contest the enemy's side jungle creeps when the minion wave is pushed.

✨ Participate in Turtle/Lord objective fights after securing your lane.

🔥 Jungle (Secondary):

✨ Standard Jungle Route: Start with the nearest Red Buff (or Blue Buff, depending on the need for mana/cooldown) then Small Jungle Creeps and Other Buff. Look for a Gank or head toward the Gold Crab/Turtle.

✨ Utilize his basic attack synergy and sustained damage to clear jungle camps efficiently.
hero.hero_strength:
👑 Laning

🔥 Early Game Bully: One of the strongest EXP Laners at Level 1-4. His passive's True Damage and Ultimate's early power spike dominate most 1v1 matchups.

🔥 Exceptional Sustain: His Ultimate provides massive HP Regen and bonus Max HP, allowing him to win extended trades and quickly recover.

🔥 Fast Wave Clear: The S1 gS2 - S1 combo allows for quick minion and jungle clearing, creating rotational pressure.

👑 Team Fight

🔥 Backline Executioner: With the Ultimate active, his high Attack Speed and True Damage shreds squishy backline heroes (Mages/MMs) quickly.

🔥 Frontline Tankiness: Becomes a high-HP, self-healing disruptor. He forces enemies to commit heavy resources (CC and Burst) to take him down.

🔥 Objective Contesting: Excellent at fighting inside the Lord/Turtle pit due to his durability and sustained damage.

👑 Hero Characteristics 

🔥 True Damage: His passive deals True Damage, making him effective against high-defense Tank/Fighter lineups without relying on Pen items.

🔥 Mana-less: Can continuously use his abilities and stay on the battlefield without running out of resources.
hero.hero_weakness:
👑 Laning

🔥 Anti-Heal Vulnerability: His entire laning sustain is crippled by early Dominance Ice or Sea Halberd from the enemy laner/jungler.

🔥 Predictable Engagement: His only reliable blink is Skill 2 (Chasm Trample), which has a relatively long cooldown. If used to engage, he has no immediate escape.

🔥 Prone to Ganks: Without his blink, he is slow and easily caught by enemy roamer/jungler rotations.

👑 Team Fight

🔥 Susceptible to Kiting: Highly dependent on his single blink skill and movement speed. Heroes with good slow/knockback can keep him at bay.

🔥 High CC Vulnerability: Hard Crowd Control (Stun/Knock-up) completely halts his basic attacks, which stops his passive True Damage, his Ultimate's Attack Speed buff, and his HP Regeneration.

🔥 Limited Utility/Hard CC: He provides no Hard CC (only slows). His utility is purely damage and zoning, which can be less valuable than a fighter who can stun multiple enemies.

👑 Hero Characteristics 

🔥 Basic Attack Dependent: The core of his damage and his healing comes from frequent basic attacks. Disarm effects will neutralize him.

🔥 Falls Off Late Game (without a lead): While his Ultimate is strong, his damage can be outscaled by fully-built Marksmen, and his lack of CC becomes a greater detriment as team fights become more decisive.

data.recommend: Dominate with Thamuz with the help of this Guide.

spell.spell_desc:
✨ For the EXP Lane, FLICKER is the most versatile and generally recommended spell, as it fixes Thamuz's main weakness: limited reliable mobility. VENGEANCE is the next best choice if you need maximum durability in team fights.

✨ For the Jungler, RETRIBUTION is best for faster farming.

counters.counter_desc: (Long-Range Poke/CC) Their range and disengage tools keep Thamuz out of his melee range, preventing him from utilizing his basic attacks to sustain.
Dyrroth's Armor Reduction and burst combo can quickly destroy Thamuz before his Ultimate healing kicks in.

dominants.dominant_desc:
Early Game (Strong)

His initial clear speed with S1 is excellent, and his innate sustain and True Damage make him a terrifying 1v1 threat in the EXP Lane. Aggressively zone the enemy laner, deny minion waves, and prioritize the Gold/XP Crab.

Mid Game (Very Strong)

Once he gets War Axe and Corrosion Scythe, his damage and chase potential are maximized. This is his prime time for successful ganks, leading objective (Turtle) pushes, and initiating smaller skirmishes.

Late Game (Moderate-Strong)

While he still deals high damage, his effectiveness starts to drop off as enemy Marksmen get their full builds and have strong Anti-Heal items. His role shifts to being a relentless disruptor/frontliner who forces the enemy to deal with him first while his team cleans up.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Swift: Grants an increase to Attack Speed. Since Thamuz's passive and Ultimate-boosted sustain are highly reliant on landing basic attacks quickly, this talent significantly boosts his damage and healing output.

Festival of Blood: Provides a huge boost to Spell Vamp, which translates directly into more HP recovery from his skills (especially his Ultimate's healing). This is the key to his legendary sustain in prolonged team fights.

Brave Smite: A viable choice for a tankier setup. Whenever Thamuz deals damage with a skill, he recovers a percentage of his Max HP. Synergizes with his frequent use of S1 and S2 resets.

equips.equip_desc:
1. Tough Boots / Warrior Boots
Tough Boots for CC Reduction/Magic Defense (most common). Warrior Boots for more Physical Defense.

2. War Axe
Core damage and CDR item. The passive stacks grant Physical Attack, Physical Penetration, and Movement Speed, which are all vital for keeping up the pressure.

3. Corrosion Scythe
CORE ATTACK SPEED. Provides Attack Speed, Physical Attack, and a passive slow effect on basic attacks, making it impossible for enemies to kite him while his Ultimate is active.

4. Oracle
Great synergy with his Ultimate and Festival of Blood. Increases all HP Regen and Shield effects, making his lifesteal/healing much stronger.

5. Brute Force Breastplate	Grants stacking Physical and Magic Defense and Movement Speed upon using skills or basic attacks, boosting his mobility and tankiness during fights.

6. Immortality / Queen's Wings
Immortality for a second chance. Queen's Wings reduces damage taken at low HP and grants more Spell Vamp, making him even harder to kill in duels.

2. War Axe

equips.equip_title: EXP Lane Focus - Spare Items Added

hero.hero_overview:
Hero Type: Fighter
Primary Role: EXP Lane (Frontliner/Sustainer)
Secondary Role: Jungler
Specialty: Regen/Burst Damage
Difficulty: Moderate (Requires good positioning and basic attack timing).

Thamuz is a relentless, short-range Fighter who thrives in prolonged battles. His revamp has shifted his focus to a more hybrid playstyle, relying on the synergy between his skills and empowered basic attacks to dish out consistent true damage and sustain his HP. He excels at 1v1 duels, zoning, and serving as a disruptive second frontline in team fights.
hero.hero_strength:
Incredible Sustain

His Ultimate, Cauterant Inferno, grants a huge HP boost, increased Attack Speed, and powerful HP regeneration with every hit (Basic Attacks, Scythes, and Grand Lord Lava procs). He is extremely difficult to kill while his Ultimate is active.

Consistent True Damage

His Passive, Grand Lord Lava, ignites the target after a few hits, dealing a burst of True Damage. This damage is difficult for tanks and durable heroes to counter.

High Mobility & Gank Potential

Chasm Trample (S2) is a reliable gap closer/escape, and its cooldown instantly resets if he retrieves his scythes, allowing for double jumps or a quick entry/exit from combat.

Excellent Wave/Objective Clear

The AoE damage from his scythes (Molten Scythes - S1) and the enhanced attack speed from his Ultimate make him one of the fastest EXP laners for clearing minion waves, jungle creeps, and securing the Turtle/Lord.
hero.hero_weakness:
Susceptible to Kiting/CC

Thamuz must stay in melee range to deal his damage and sustain. Heroes with high Crowd Control (CC) like stun/knock-up or heroes with high mobility and long-range poke can easily stop him from landing his basic attacks, effectively shutting down his healing and damage output.

Vulnerable without Ultimate

His power spike is entirely dependent on his Ultimate. Without it, his damage and sustain are significantly lower, making him easy to defeat.

Anti-Heal Dependency

His massive HP regeneration is easily countered by item effects like Sea Halberd or Dominance Ice, which heavily reduce his healing.

Prediction-reliant Mobility

To maximize his mobility, he must correctly predict where to throw his Molten Scythes (S1) so he can follow up with a reset on Chasm Trample (S2). Missing the retrieval leaves his S2 on a long cooldown.

Increases his already high durability by reducing incoming damage and reflecting magic damage back, turning him into a terrifying damage sponge during his Ultimate.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Thamuz synergizes within Mobile Legends: Bang Bang. Thamuz is a fighter hero known for his aggressive, sustained damage, and self-sustaining playstyle. His synergy can be observed both within his own kit and with team compositions.

counters.counter_desc: - Scythe Management and Empowered Basic Attacks: Thamuz's passive, Grand Lord Lava, is central to his damage output. When his scythes are not with him, he gains increased movement speed. Retrieving them empowers his next basic attack, dealing extra damage, slowing the target, and creating a lava gush that explodes for additional physical damage. This synergizes directly with Slaughterous Scythes (Skill 1), which throws his scythes, and Chasm Leap (Skill 2), which allows him to jump and retrieve them. Players constantly cycle through throwing scythes, repositioning, and retrieving them to maximize empowered basic attacks .

dominants.dominant_desc: Thamuz, the Lord Lava, is a fighter hero known for his aggressive playstyle and sustained damage output. Here are some tricks to maximize his potential:

emblems.emblem_desc: - Physical Attack Boost: Thamuz relies heavily on physical damage, and the Fighter emblem provides a significant boost to his physical attack, increasing the damage output of his skills and basic attacks.

equips.equip_desc: - Damage: Thamuz excels at dealing sustained damage over time with his skills and passive. A fighter build ensures he has enough physical attack and penetration to be a significant threat to enemy heroes.

hero.hero_overview: Thamuz is a Fighter hero who specializes in chase and damage, typically played in the EXP lane. He is known for dominating the early game and *********** if not dealt with properly .
hero.hero_strength:
- Early Game Dominance: Thamuz excels in the early game, bullying his lane opponents with his Skill 1, which is a potent zoning tool .

- High Damage and Chase Potential: He deals high damage and is excellent at chasing enemies .

- Area of Effect Damage: Thamuz's skills deal area-of-effect damage, making him effective in team fights .

- Sustain: His ultimate provides lifesteal, allowing him to restore HP with basic attacks .

- Good at Farming: Thamuz farms well, allowing him to acquire necessary items quickly .
hero.hero_weakness:
- Susceptible to Crowd Control: Thamuz is vulnerable to crowd control effects, which can interrupt his skills and movement .

- Weak in Team Fights: Despite his AoE damage, Thamuz is considered weak in team fights .

- Loses Potency Late Game: Thamuz's effectiveness diminishes in the late game as enemies can outrun him and negate his true damage passive

hero.hero_overview:
Thamuz is a fighter hero in Mobile Legends known for his continuous burn damage and strong sustain. His passive applies lava burn on enemies with each attack, making him deadly in extended trades. With Molten Scythes (Skill 1) for zoning and poking, and Chasm Trample (Skill 2) for mobility and knock-up, he can control fights and chase down enemies effectively.

His Ultimate, Cauterant Inferno, makes him a powerful frontline presence by dealing AoE burn damage while restoring his HP through lifesteal. This allows him to survive long teamfights and pressure the enemy backline.

Thamuz excels in the EXP Lane, where he can dominate 1v1 duels, but he can also be played as a jungler due to his fast clear speed and strong early game pressure. He is best paired with roamers that provide hard crowd control like Atlas or Tigreal, and he struggles most against heroes with strong burst or kiting abilities like Valir and Karrie.
hero.hero_strength:
Deals continuous burn damage with his passive and ultimate, making him strong in long fights.

Has great sustain from lifesteal, allowing him to survive in teamfights.

Fast wave and jungle clear, giving him early game pressure.

Dominates 1v1 duels and is excellent at securing Turtle and Lord.

Can be played flexibly as EXP Laner or Jungler.
hero.hero_weakness:
Lacks strong crowd control, relying only on a small knock-up and slow.

Can be kited by ranged heroes and pushed away from fights.

Weak against burst damage that kills him before he can lifesteal.

Needs items to become effective, making him vulnerable in the early game.

Ultimate-dependent — less threatening when his Cauterant Inferno is on cooldown.

spell.spell_desc: Thamuz has no dash or mobility skill, which makes him vulnerable to kiting from ranged heroes. Flicker solves this weakness by giving him instant mobility for both offense and defense.
